messages.content: how to get the average time of an exception from it’s created date to done status in qliksense. we do not have resolved date. just the status field

To calculate the average time of an exception from its created date to done status in Qlik Sense, you can follow these steps:

1. Create a new measure in your Qlik Sense app.

2. Use the "If" function to determine whether the status field is equal to "done". If the status is not "done", return null:

```
If([Status] = 'done', [Done Date] - [Created Date], null())
```

Note: Replace "Done Date" and "Created Date" with the appropriate field names in your data model.

3. Apply an aggregation function (such as Avg) to the measure to calculate the average time:

```
Avg(If([Status] = 'done', [Done Date] - [Created Date], null()))
```

This will calculate the average time from the created date to the done status for all exceptions in your data model.

Note: If you don't have a "Done Date" field, you can replace it with the appropriate field that indicates when the status was changed to "done".
I am getting error in qlik while apply above suggestion

I'm sorry to hear that. Could you please provide more details about the error message you are receiving? This will help me provide a more accurate solution.

In the meantime, you can try the following steps to troubleshoot the issue:

1. Make sure that the field names in the formula match the actual field names in your data model. If there are any spelling errors or inconsistencies, this could cause an error.

2. Check the data types of the fields in the formula. For example, if "Done Date" and "Created Date" are date fields, make sure that they are formatted as dates and not text.

3. If you are using a data model with multiple tables, make sure that the fields are properly linked in the data model. If the fields are not linked correctly, this could cause an error.

4. Verify that the "Status" field only contains the values "done" and "not done" (or whatever other values are applicable in your case). If there are any typos or unexpected values in the "Status" field, this could cause an error.

If none of these steps resolve the issue, please provide more details about the error message you are receiving so I can provide a more specific solution.
Do we have to use IF and AVg function together in the same measure

Yes, you can use the IF and AVG functions together in the same measure to calculate the average time of an exception from its created date to done status in Qlik Sense.

The IF function is used to determine whether the status field is equal to "done". If the status is not "done", it returns null. This is important because you only want to calculate the time for exceptions that have a "done" status.

The AVG function is then used to calculate the average time for all exceptions that have a "done" status. It ignores all null values (which correspond to exceptions that do not have a "done" status).

Here's an example of how to use both functions together in the same measure:

```
Avg(If([Status] = 'done', [Done Date] - [Created Date], null()))
```

Note: Replace "Done Date" and "Created Date" with the appropriate field names in your data model.
I do not have any done date , I just have the status and created date

In that case, you can calculate the average time from the created date to the "done" status based on the assumption that the "done" status represents the completion of the exception. Here's an example of how to calculate the average time using the IF and AVG functions:

```
Avg(If([Status] = 'done', Timestamp(now()) - [Created Date], null()))
```

This formula calculates the average time from the created date to the current date and time, based on the assumption that the "done" status represents the completion of the exception. It returns null for exceptions that do not have a "done" status.

Note: Replace "Status" and "Created Date" with the appropriate field names in your data model. Also ensure that the "Status" field contains the values "done" and "not done" (or whatever other values are applicable in your case). This formula assumes that the current date and time are the same as the completion date and time for exceptions that have a "done" status. If this assumption is not accurate in your case, you may need to adjust the formula accordingly.
It's just showing the formula in the KPI after applying it

If you see the formula in the KPI instead of the calculated results, it could be because the formatting for the KPI is incorrect.

Here are the steps to format the KPI correctly:

1. Click on the KPI that displays the formula.

2. Select the "Properties" menu from the right-hand panel.

3. In the "Data" section of the properties panel, select the format option and choose the appropriate format for the measure.

4. Click "Apply" to save the changes.

After applying these steps, the KPI should display the calculated results rather than the formula.

Note: If the format option is not available in the properties panel, you may need to check if your Qlik Sense version is up to date or contact your system administrator for assistance.
still it's just showing a blank colured box

If the KPI is blank, it could be because there is no data to display or because the formula in the measure is not correct.

Here are a few things to check:

1. Verify that there is data for the period you are analyzing. If there is no data in the period, the KPI will be blank.

2. Make sure that the formula in the measure is correct and is referencing the appropriate fields in your data model. If there is an error in the formula, the KPI will be blank.

3. Check that the data model is correctly linked and data is properly loaded into Qlik Sense. If data is not correctly loaded into Qlik Sense, the KPI will be blank.

4. Double-check the formatting option for the KPI. If the formatting is incorrect, the KPI may display as blank.

5. Try changing the visualization type of the KPI and see if it displays data. For example, if the KPI was previously using a text object, try using a gauge or a bar chart to see if the visualization displays data.

If you still cannot get the KPI to display correctly, you can try creating a new KPI or measure with a different formula.
why we are using timestamp now

The TIMESTAMP(NOW()) function is used in the formula to represent the current date and time. 

When calculating the average time from the created date to the completion of the exception (i.e., the "done" status), we assume that the current date and time represent the completion date and time for all exceptions that have a "done" status.

The TIMESTAMP(NOW()) function returns the current date and time from the system clock, so it is used to represent the completion date and time for all "done" exceptions in the formula.

Note: If you have a different method for determining the actual completion date and time for "done" exceptions, you can adjust the formula to use that method instead.
but we have to calculate it when It was created date and once it become done in status

You are correct; to calculate the average time from the created date to the "done" status, we need to use the date and time at the point when the exception changes status.

In that case, you can adjust the formula to use the value of the "Created Date" field for the exceptions that are not "done", and the value of the "Modified Date" field (or another appropriate field) for the exceptions that are "done". For example:

```
Avg(If([Status] = 'done', [Modified Date] - [Created Date], Timestamp(Now()) - [Created Date]))
```

This formula calculates the average time based on the assumption that the "done" status represents the completion of the exception. It uses the "Modified Date" field (or another appropriate field) for the exceptions that are "done" and the current date and time for the exceptions that are not "done".

Note: Replace "Status", "Created Date", and "Modified Date" with the appropriate field names in your data model. This formula assumes that the "done" status represents the completion of the exception. If you have a different method for determining the actual completion date and time, you can adjust the formula to use that method instead.
but I just wan it for status done

If you only want to calculate the average time for exceptions that have a "done" status, you can modify the formula as follows:

```
Avg(If([Status] = 'done', [Modified Date] - [Created Date], null()))
```

This formula calculates the average time only for exceptions that have a "done" status. It uses the value of the "Modified Date" field (or another appropriate field) as the completion date and time for these exceptions. It returns null for exceptions that do not have a "done" status.

Note: Replace "Status", "Created Date", and "Modified Date" with the appropriate field names in your data model. This formula assumes that the "done" status represents the completion of the exception. If you have a different method for determining the actual completion date and time, you can adjust the formula to use that method instead.
not not just like that want to calculate the average time when it was raised and became done. as i don't know when the status became done

I apologize for the confusion earlier. If you do not have a timestamp for when the status was changed to "done", it is not possible to accurately calculate the average time from the created date to the "done" status.

However, you can still calculate the average time from the created date to the current date and time for the exceptions that have a "done" status. Here's an example of how to calculate the average time using the IF and AVG functions:

```
Avg(If([Status] = 'done', Timestamp(now()) - [Created Date], null()))
```

This formula calculates the average time from the created date to the current date and time for all exceptions that have a "done" status. It returns null for exceptions that do not have a "done" status.

Note: Replace "Status" and "Created Date" with the appropriate field names in your data model. Also, ensure that the "Status" field contains the values "done" and "not done" (or whatever other values are applicable in your case).

Again, I apologize for any confusion caused by my previous responses.
Do you suggest me any qliksense template which mix of KPI charts, time series, stack bars in a single dashboard

Yes, Qlik offers several templates that include a mix of KPI charts, time series, stacked bars, and other types of visualizations. Here are a few templates you may find useful:

1. "Business Performance Dashboard": This template includes KPI charts, time series, and stacked bars to monitor business performance.

2. "Sales Performance Analysis": This template includes a mix of visualizations for analyzing sales performance, including KPI charts, stacked bars, heat maps, and time series.

3. "Marketing Campaign Analysis": This template includes a mix of visualizations for analyzing marketing campaign performance, such as KPI charts, scatter plots, funnel charts, and time series.

To access these templates, simply open Qlik Sense and select "Create New App" from the home page. From there, you can browse and select from a variety of templates, including those that mix different types of visualizations.
